devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । इति भागवतः पृष्टः क्षत्त्रा वार्तां प्रियाश्रयाम् । प्रतिवक्तुं न चोत्सेह औत्कण्ठ्यात् स्मारितेश्वरः ॥ १ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvācaiti bhāgavataḥ pṛṣṭaḥ kṣattrā vārtāṃ priyāśrayāmprativaktuṃ na cotseha autkaṇṭhyāt smāriteśvaraḥ
translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
The great devotee of the Lord (Uddhava) who was thus asked by Vidura about the welfare pertaining to the dear one (i.e. of Lord Kṛṣṇa), could not reply as he was painfully reminded of the Lord and was overcome with sorrow at his bereavement.

devanagari: यः पञ्चहायनो मात्रा प्रातराशाय याचितः । तन्नैच्छत् रचयन् यस्य सपर्यां बाललीलया ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: yaḥ pañcahāyano mātrā prātarāśāya yācitaḥtannaicchat racayan yasya saparyāṃ bālalīlayā
translation: How could he (Uddhava) who, as a child of five years, when invited by his mother for breakfast, did not wish to partake of it while worshipping Kṛṣṇa as a part of his sports in childhood, break the news in reply, when he had spent the whole lifetime in his service, and attained old age, and was reminded (sorrowfully) of the feet of the master.

devanagari: स कथं सेवया तस्य कालेन जरसं गतः । पृष्टो वार्तां प्रतिब्रूयाद् भर्तुः पादौ अनुस्मरन् ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: sa kathaṃ sevayā tasya kālena jarasaṃ gataḥpṛṣṭo vārtāṃ pratibrūyād bhartuḥ pādau anusmaran
translation: How could he (Uddhava) who, as a child of five years, when invited by his mother for breakfast, did not wish to partake of it while worshipping Kṛṣṇa as a part of his sports in childhood, break the news in reply, when he had spent the whole lifetime in his service, and attained old age, and was reminded (sorrowfully) of the feet of the master.

devanagari: स मुहूर्तं अभूत्तूष्णीं कृष्णाङ्घ्रिसुधया भृशम् । तीव्रेण भक्तियोगेन निमग्नः साधु निर्वृतः ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: sa muhūrtaṃ abhūttūṣṇīṃ kṛṣṇāṅghrisudhayā bhṛśamtīvreṇa bhaktiyogena nimagnaḥ sādhu nirvṛtaḥ
translation: For the period of a muhūrta he remained silent (in meditation) highly satisfied by the nectar-like feet of Kṛṣṇa and deeply immersed in intense devotion of the Lord.

devanagari: पुलकोद् भिन्नसर्वाङ्गो मुञ्चन्मीलद्दृशा शुचः । पूर्णार्थो लक्षितस्तेन स्नेहप्रसरसम्प्लुतः ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: pulakod bhinnasarvāṅgo muñcanmīladdṛśā śucaḥpūrṇārtho lakṣitastena snehaprasarasamplutaḥ
translation: He, on whose body hair was standing on ends, and who was shedding tears from his closed eyes, and who was drowned in the flood of love for Kṛṣṇa, was seen (as if) his highest purpose was achieved.

devanagari: शनकैः भगवल्लोकान् नृलोकं पुनरागतः । विमृज्य नेत्रे विदुरं प्रीत्याहोद्धव उत्स्मयन् ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: śanakaiḥ bhagavallokān nṛlokaṃ punarāgataḥvimṛjya netre viduraṃ prītyāhoddhava utsmayan
translation: Gradually, he returned from the abode of the Lord to the world of men. He wiped out his eyes. Uddhava who was wonḍerstruck (with the deeds of the Lord) replied to Vidura.

devanagari: उद्धव उवाच । कृष्णद्युमणि निम्लोचे गीर्णेष्वजगरेण ह । किं नु नः कुशलं ब्रूयां गतश्रीषु गृहेष्वहम् ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: uddhava uvācakṛṣṇadyumaṇi nimloce gīrṇeṣvajagareṇa hakiṃ nu naḥ kuśalaṃ brūyāṃ gataśrīṣu gṛheṣvaham
translation:
Uddhava said:
What good news can I give to you when the Sun in the form of Kṛṣṇa has set, and our homes, bereft of splendour, have been swallowed up by the boa-constriction (of Time)?

devanagari: दुर्भगो बत लोकोऽयं यदवो नितरामपि । ये संवसन्तो न विदुः हरिं मीना इवोडुपम् ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: durbhago bata loko'yaṃ yadavo nitarāmapiye saṃvasanto na viduḥ hariṃ mīnā ivoḍupam
translation: Alas! it is the misfortune of this world, and es serially of the Yādavas who lived with him intimately and yet did not know him, just as the fish (living in the sea along with the moon before the churning of the sea) did not recognize the Moon (as such but regarded him as an acquatic animal).

devanagari: इङ्गितज्ञाः पुरुप्रौढा एकारामाश्च सात्वताः । सात्वतां ऋषभं सर्वे भूतावासममंसत ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: iṅgitajñāḥ puruprauḍhā ekārāmāśca sātvatāḥsātvatāṃ ṛṣabhaṃ sarve bhūtāvāsamamaṃsata
translation: [1] All the Sāttvatas, though capable of knowing intentions of others, and possessing deep understanding, and of singleness of devotion, regarded the Lord of all created beings[2] as the leader of the Sāttvatas only.
purport:
Footnote [1]:
According to Siddhāntapradīpa this verse praises the Yādavas and Pauravas who knew the real Lord. “The leaders of the Puru-clan (like Yudhiṣṭhira) and Sāttvatas (like Vasudeva) realised the chief of Sāttvatas, namely, Śrī Kṛṣṇa as being the Brahman, the support of all created beings (bhūtas) and resting completely on the Lord, were happy thereby.”

Footnote [2]:
bhūtāvāsam [bhūtāvāsa]: (i) A jīva, resident in a physical and inner (kāraṇa) body—Subodhinī
(ii) A leader of a few men—Padaratnāvalī

devanagari: देवस्य मायया स्पृष्टा ये चान्यद् असदाश्रिताः । भ्राम्यते धीर्न तद्वाक्यैः आत्मन्युप्तात्मनो हरौ ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: devasya māyayā spṛṣṭā ye cānyad asadāśritāḥbhrāmyate dhīrna tadvākyaiḥ ātmanyuptātmano harau
translation: Those who have sown (i.e. completely fixed) their intellect in Han, are not deluded by the words of those who are haunted by the Māyā of the Almighty (e.g. the Yādavas) or by those who entertained evil intention to him (like Śiśupāla and others).

devanagari: प्रदर्श्या तप्ततपसां अवितृप्तदृशां नृणाम् । आदायान्तरधाद्यस्तु स्वबिम्बं लोकलोचनम् ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: pradarśyā taptatapasāṃ avitṛptadṛśāṃ nṛṇāmādāyāntaradhādyastu svabimbaṃ lokalocanam
translation: He had exhibited his form to men who had not performed proper penance and who had not seen it to their satisfaction. He disappeared from the world taking with him his splendid form which was the centre (of attraction) to the eyes of the world.

devanagari: यन्मर्त्यलीलौपयिकं स्वयोग । मायाबलं दर्शयता गृहीतम् । विस्मापनं स्वस्य च सौभगर्द्धेः । परं पदं भूषणभूषणाङ्गम् ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: yanmartyalīlaupayikaṃ svayogamāyābalaṃ darśayatā gṛhītamvismāpanaṃ svasya ca saubhagarddheḥparaṃ padaṃ bhūṣaṇabhūṣaṇāṅgam
translation: It was a form which was useful for activities in imitation of human beings which he assumed, showing the power of his Yoga-māyā. It was the highest peak of perfect beauty and sublimity, parts of which beautified even the ornaments and a wonder to him also.

devanagari: यद्धर्मसूनोर्बत राजसूये । निरीक्ष्य दृक्स्वस्त्ययनं त्रिलोकः । कार्त्स्न्येन चाद्येह गतं विधातुः । अर्वाक्सृतौ कौशलमित्यमन्यत ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: yaddharmasūnorbata rājasūyenirīkṣya dṛksvastyayanaṃ trilokaḥkārtsnyena cādyeha gataṃ vidhātuḥarvāksṛtau kauśalamityamanyata
translation: Seeing his form which gave great delight to the three worlds in the Rājasūya sacrifice of Yudhiṣṭhira, they (people of the three worlds) thought that in creating beautiful human form, the whole of creator’s skill is exhausted today.

devanagari: यस्यानुरागप्लुतहासरास । लीलावलोकप्रतिलब्धमानाः । व्रजस्त्रियो दृग्भिरनुप्रवृत्त । धियोऽवतस्थुः किल कृत्यशेषाः ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: yasyānurāgaplutahāsarāsalīlāvalokapratilabdhamānāḥvrajastriyo dṛgbhiranupravṛttadhiyo'vatasthuḥ kila kṛtyaśeṣāḥ
translation: The women of Vraja who got the honour of his smiles overflowing with love, sportive dances and longing looks, followed him with their eyes and minds, leaving their work unfinished.

devanagari: स्वशान्तरूपेष्वितरैः स्वरूपैः । अभ्यर्द्यमानेष्वनुकम्पितात्मा । परावरेशो महदंशयुक्तो । ह्यजोऽपि जातो भगवान् यथाग्निः ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: svaśāntarūpeṣvitaraiḥ svarūpaiḥabhyardyamāneṣvanukampitātmāparāvareśo mahadaṃśayuktohyajo'pi jāto bhagavān yathāgniḥ
translation: The Lord of all, [Brahman with attributes and beyond attributes] with compassion for his own gentle forms being troubled by other (wicked) ones, and accompanied with Prakṛti of which Mahat is but a fraction, (or with Balarāma as his great part), though unborn, took birth like fire (which though ever present in the wood, sparks out after friction).

devanagari: मां खेदयत्येतदजस्य जन्म । विडम्बनं यद्वसुदेवगेहे । व्रजे च वासोऽरिभयादिव स्वयं । पुराद् व्यवात्सीद् यत् अनन्तवीर्यः ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: māṃ khedayatyetadajasya janmaviḍambanaṃ yadvasudevagehevraje ca vāso'ribhayādiva svayaṃpurād vyavātsīd yat anantavīryaḥ
translation: The apparent birth of the birthless Lord in the house of Vasudeva, (his) stay in a settlement of cowherds (Vraja) as if out of fear of the enemy (Kaṃsa), and the flight of the Lord of infinite power, from his own city—this (behaviour like ordinary human beings) distresses me.

devanagari: दुनोति चेतः स्मरतो ममैतद् । यदाह पादावभिवन्द्य पित्रोः । ताताम्ब कंसाद् उरुशंकितानां । प्रसीदतं नोऽकृतनिष्कृतीनाम् ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: dunoti cetaḥ smarato mamaitadyadāha pādāvabhivandya pitroḥtātāmba kaṃsād uruśaṃkitānāṃprasīdataṃ no'kṛtaniṣkṛtīnām
translation: It pains my heart to remember how he, bowing down to the feet of his parents, beseeched, “Oh father, mother kindly be gracious unto us who being in great fear of Kaṃsa,. could not render you any service.”

devanagari: को वा अमुष्याङ्घ्रिसरोजरेणुं । विस्मर्तुमीशीत पुमान् विजिघ्रन् । यो विस्फुरद्‍भ्रूविटपेन भूमेः । भारं कृतान्तेन तिरश्चकार ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: ko vā amuṣyāṅghrisarojareṇuṃvismartumīśīta pumān vijighranyo visphurad‍bhrūviṭapena bhūmeḥbhāraṃ kṛtāntena tiraścakāra
translation: What man who having smelt (experienced the great joy in) even a particle of pollen in the lotus-like feet of the Lord, can forget him—the Lord, who with the movement of his creeper-like eyebrow, a veritable god of destruction, removed the load (of the wicked) from the earth.

devanagari: दृष्टा भवद्भिः ननु राजसूये । चैद्यस्य कृष्णं द्विषतोऽपि सिद्धिः । यां योगिनः संस्पृहयन्ति सम्यग् । योगेन कस्तद्विरहं सहेत ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: dṛṣṭā bhavadbhiḥ nanu rājasūyecaidyasya kṛṣṇaṃ dviṣato'pi siddhiḥyāṃ yoginaḥ saṃspṛhayanti samyagyogena kastadvirahaṃ saheta
translation: Verily, you have witnessed how, during the Rājasūya Sacrifice, Śiśupāla, the arch-enemy of Kṛṣṇa, attained Liberation after which even yogins aspire by observing carefully yogic practices. How can one endure separation from (such a gracious Lord like) him?

devanagari: तथैव चान्ये नरलोकवीरा । य आहवे कृष्णमुखारविन्दम् । नेत्रैः पिबन्तो नयनाभिरामं । पार्थास्त्रपूताः पदमापुरस्य ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: tathaiva cānye naralokavīrāya āhave kṛṣṇamukhāravindamnetraiḥ pibanto nayanābhirāmaṃpārthāstrapūtāḥ padamāpurasya
translation: Similarly, other warriors who, on the field of battle, drank with their eyes the beautiful lotus-like face of Kṛṣṇa, and attained to his feet after purification (death) from the missiles of Arjuna.

devanagari: स्वयं त्वसाम्यातिशयस्त्र्यधीशः । स्वाराज्यलक्ष्म्याप्तसमस्तकामः । बलिं हरद्भिश्चिरलोकपालैः । किरीटकोट्येडितपादपीठः ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: svayaṃ tvasāmyātiśayastryadhīśaḥsvārājyalakṣmyāptasamastakāmaḥbaliṃ haradbhiściralokapālaiḥkirīṭakoṭyeḍitapādapīṭhaḥ
translation: The Lord, who himself is unsurpassed in excellence (lit. without an equal or superior), and is a ruler of the three (worlds or attributes—guṇas), and who due to his intrinsic highest blissfulness, is in enjoyment of all (types of) happiness, whose foot-stool is (as if) eulogised by (the sound on the foot-stool made by) the crests of the crowṇs of the eternal guardians of the world who bring tributes to him—Such a Lord should stand before Ugrasena who is occupying a high seat (on the throne), and should request him, “Your Majesty, kindly pay attention (to us)Oh Viḍura, the servant-like behaviour of the Lord greatly troubles us, his servants.

devanagari: तत्तस्य कैङ्कर्यमलं भृतान्नो । विग्लापयत्यङ्ग यदुग्रसेनम् । तिष्ठन्निषण्णं परमेष्ठिधिष्ण्ये । न्यबोधयद्देव निधारयेति ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: tattasya kaiṅkaryamalaṃ bhṛtānnoviglāpayatyaṅga yadugrasenamtiṣṭhanniṣaṇṇaṃ parameṣṭhidhiṣṇyenyabodhayaddeva nidhārayeti
translation: The Lord, who himself is unsurpassed in excellence (lit. without an equal or superior), and is a ruler of the three (worlds or attributes—guṇas), and who due to his intrinsic highest blissfulness, is in enjoyment of all (types of) happiness, whose foot-stool is (as if) eulogised by (the sound on the foot-stool made by) the crests of the crowṇs of the eternal guardians of the world who bring tributes to him—Such a Lord should stand before Ugrasena who is occupying a high seat (on the throne), and should request him, “Your Majesty, kindly pay attention (to us)Oh Viḍura, the servant-like behaviour of the Lord greatly troubles us, his servants.

devanagari: अहो बकी यं स्तनकालकूटं । जिघांसयापाययदप्यसाध्वी । लेभे गतिं धात्र्युचितां ततोऽन्यं । कं वा दयालुं शरणं व्रजेम ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: aho bakī yaṃ stanakālakūṭaṃjighāṃsayāpāyayadapyasādhvīlebhe gatiṃ dhātryucitāṃ tato'nyaṃkaṃ vā dayāluṃ śaraṇaṃ vrajema
translation: What mercifulness! The wicked Pūtanā who with a desire to kill him, made him suck her breast smeared with deadly poison, (and for this she) attained the place worthy for his nurse (Yaśodā) Is there any other god so merciful like this, to whom we should resort (for salvation or protection?

devanagari: मन्येऽसुरान् भागवतांस्त्र्यधीशे । संरम्भमार्गाभिनिविष्टचित्तान् । ये संयुगेऽचक्षत तार्क्ष्यपुत्र । मंसे सुनाभायुधमापतन्तम् ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: manye'surān bhāgavatāṃstryadhīśesaṃrambhamārgābhiniviṣṭacittānye saṃyuge'cakṣata tārkṣyaputramaṃse sunābhāyudhamāpatantam
translation: I regard Asuras as the devotees of the Supreme Lord as their minds are fixed on the Lord of the three worlds, through anger and who on the battlefield visualized him armed with the Sudarśana disc and seated on the shoulder of Garuḍa, attacking them.

devanagari: वसुदेवस्य देवक्यां जातो भोजेन्द्रबन्धने । चिकीर्षुर्भगवानस्याः शमजेनाभियाचितः ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: vasudevasya devakyāṃ jāto bhojendrabandhanecikīrṣurbhagavānasyāḥ śamajenābhiyācitaḥ
translation: The Lord took birth from Vasudeva and Devakī, in the prison of Kaṃsa, the chief of the Bhojas, as he was so requested by god Brahmā and desired to restore happiness to the earth.

devanagari: ततो नन्दव्रजमितः पित्रा कंसाद् विबिभ्यता । एकादश समास्तत्र गूढार्चिः सबलोऽवसत् ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: tato nandavrajamitaḥ pitrā kaṃsād vibibhyatāekādaśa samāstatra gūḍhārciḥ sabalo'vasat
translation: Then, by his father who was afraid of Kaṃsa, he was taken to the settlement of cowherds headed by Nanda. He stayed there with Balarāma with his power unmanifested, for eleven years.

devanagari: परीतो वत्सपैर्वत्सान् चारयन् व्यहरद्विभुः । यमुनोपवने कूजद् द्विजसङ्कुलिताङ्घ्रिपे ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: parīto vatsapairvatsān cārayan vyaharadvibhuḥyamunopavane kūjad dvijasaṅkulitāṅghripe
translation: Surrounded by cowboys and himself tending the calves, the All-pervading Lord sported on the (banks of the) Yamunā in parks, dense with trees full of warbling birds.

devanagari: कौमारीं दर्शयन् चेष्टां प्रेक्षणीयां व्रजौकसाम् । रुदन्निव हसन्मुग्ध बालसिंहावलोकनः ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: kaumārīṃ darśayan ceṣṭāṃ prekṣaṇīyāṃ vrajaukasāmrudanniva hasanmugdha bālasiṃhāvalokanaḥ
translation: He who looked like an innocent young one of a lion, displayed his boyish pranks of (apparent) laughing and crying, to the residents of Vraja.

devanagari: स एव गोधनं लक्ष्म्या निकेतं सितगोवृषम् । चारयन्ननुगान् गोपान् रणद् वेणुररीरमत् ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: sa eva godhanaṃ lakṣmyā niketaṃ sitagovṛṣamcārayannanugān gopān raṇad veṇurarīramat
translation: The same Lord tended their wealth in the from of cattle with white bulls (prominent in them) and which was (as if), the abode of the goddess of wealth. He entertained the cowherds accompanying him, by playing upon the flute.

devanagari: प्रयुक्तान् भोजराजेन मायिनः कामरूपिणः । लीलया व्यनुदत्तान् तान् बालः क्रीडनकानिव ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: prayuktān bhojarājena māyinaḥ kāmarūpiṇaḥlīlayā vyanudattān tān bālaḥ krīḍanakāniva
translation: Like a boy breaking down his toys, he sportively killed those wiley conjuring demons who could assume any form at will and who were deputed by Kaṃsa (to kill him).

devanagari: विपन्नान् विषपानेन निगृह्य भुजगाधिपम् । उत्थाप्यापाययद् गावः तत्तोयं प्रकृतिस्थितम् ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: vipannān viṣapānena nigṛhya bhujagādhipamutthāpyāpāyayad gāvaḥ tattoyaṃ prakṛtisthitam
translation: He brought back to life the cowherds and cows who died of poisonous water of the Yamunā. Subduing (and expelling) Kāliya, the king of serpents, he made them drink the water again which was restored to its original condition, of purity.

devanagari: अयाजयद् गोसवेन गोपराजं द्विजोत्तमैः । वित्तस्य चोरुभारस्य चिकीर्षन् सद्व्ययं विभुः ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: ayājayad gosavena goparājaṃ dvijottamaiḥvittasya corubhārasya cikīrṣan sadvyayaṃ vibhuḥ
translation: The omnipresent Lord made the chief of cowherds (Nanda) to perform a sacrifice in honour of cows (in which cows were worshipped) with the help of the best of Brāhmaṇas, as he wished to spend the great wealth in a good way. (In. this way, he subdued the pride of Indra).

devanagari: वर्षतीन्द्रे व्रजः कोपाद् भग्नमानेऽतिविह्वलः । गोत्रलीलातपत्रेण त्रातो भद्रानुगृह्णता ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: varṣatīndre vrajaḥ kopād bhagnamāne'tivihvalaḥgotralīlātapatreṇa trāto bhadrānugṛhṇatā
translation: Oh Vidura! When Indra, incensed at the discontinuation of his own (traditional) worship, showered heavily, the settlement of the cowherds which became extremely distressed (by the downpour of rain), was protected (by the Lord) with the umbrella in the form of the hill (Govardhana) which he sportively wielded and showed thereby his grace.

devanagari: शरच्छशिकरैर्मृष्टं मानयन् रजनीमुखम् । गायन् कलपदं रेमे स्त्रीणां मण्डलमण्डनः ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: śaracchaśikarairmṛṣṭaṃ mānayan rajanīmukhamgāyan kalapadaṃ reme strīṇāṃ maṇḍalamaṇḍanaḥ
translation:
Respecting (as it were) the evenings bright with autumnal moon, he enjoyed himself singing sweet songs and became an ornament of the circle of ladies (performing Rāsa dance).
